bead.title: Probe Qwen3.6-27B + populate catalog + fix surface-id lookup + revert benchmark profiles
bead.description:
Operator-driven integration bead that depends on the schema bead, probe-tool
bead, and translator bead all landing. Executes the probe against
Qwen3.6-27B on all four lanes, writes the verdict into the catalog, fixes the
surface-id lookup mismatch, and reverts the benchmark profiles to use named
tier shortcuts.

Steps (must land as a single commit set — partial state regresses runs):

1. Run the probe against each of:
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-ds4.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-qwen3-6-27b.yaml (legacy oMLX; optional
     if vidar is on ds4 only)
   Save artifacts under docs/research/probe-reasoning-qwen3-6-27b-2026-05-11/.

2. Update internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml `qwen3.6-27b` entry:
   - Set `reasoning_wire` based on the probe verdict. Expected (per
     ADR-010 evidence): `tokens` on OpenRouter; `tokens` on the Qwen-native
     lanes (since they only express a budget anyway, this normalizes the
     classification).
   - Today the entry has `reasoning_wire: model_id` which is wrong (Qwen3.6
     does not encode reasoning level in its model name on OR; that flag was
     inherited from the qwen3.5-27b entry). Replace with the probe-driven
     value.
   - Keep `reasoning_levels`, `reasoning_control`, `reasoning_budgets`
     unchanged — they're orthogonal to wire form.

3. Fix internal/config/config.go:925-944 `modelReasoningWireMap()`:
   - The function today keys by catalog ID. The provider queries by surface
     ID. They mismatch for any model where catalog ID != surface ID (e.g.
     `qwen3.6-27b` vs `qwen/qwen3.6-27b`).
   - Refactor to return a map[surfaceSystem]map[surfaceID]string, e.g.
     {"agent.openai": {"qwen/qwen3.6-27b": "tokens"}}.
   - Update the provider construction sites that consume it
     (internal/provider/openai/registration.go:29,
     internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go:34) to pass the
     surface-specific submap.
   - Add a test in internal/config/config_test.go that asserts the lookup
     resolves via the surface ID, not the catalog ID, for Qwen3.6 on OR.

4. Revert all four Qwen + ds4 benchmark profiles from `reasoning: 4096` to a
   named tier — `reasoning: low` is the right default per the OR truncated-
   fail wall analysis (the probe should confirm low ~= 2048 reasoning_tokens,
   which is below Qwen's ~4500 nominal `low` observation):
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-ds4.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
   Update the inline profile comments to reference ADR-010 and reflect that
   the catalog now owns the wire shape.

5. Run a single-cell sweep against one TerminalBench-2.1 task on each lane to
   verify report.json carries reasoning_tokens > 0 and that the value sits in
   the expected band per the probe verdict.

In-scope files:
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml
- internal/config/config.go
- internal/config/config_test.go
- internal/provider/openai/registration.go (lookup-call-site update)
- internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go (lookup-call-site update)
- scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
- scripts/benchmark/profiles/sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.yaml
- scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-ds4.yaml
- scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
- docs/research/probe-reasoning-qwen3-6-27b-2026-05-11/ (probe artifacts)

Out-of-scope:
- Other Qwen models in the catalog (qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-235b-a22b, etc.) —
  one fish at a time; file follow-ups if probing reveals divergent behavior.
- Non-Qwen models on OR (GPT-5, Claude, etc.) — defer until a real divergence
  shows up; they're served well by the wire="provider" backwards-compat path.
bead.acceptance:
1. Probe artifacts exist under docs/research/probe-reasoning-qwen3-6-27b-
   2026-05-11/ with one subdirectory per lane, each containing per-matrix-
   point request and response bodies plus a verdict summary.
2. internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml qwen3.6-27b entry has
   reasoning_wire set to a value driven by the probe (expected `tokens` but
   probe is the authority).
3. `go test ./internal/config/...` passes including a new test that
   modelReasoningWireMap resolves wire form by surface ID for
   `qwen/qwen3.6-27b` on `agent.openai`, returning the catalog value
   (not "").
4. All four benchmark profiles list `reasoning: low` (or whichever named tier
   the operator picks based on the probe) and have updated comments
   referencing ADR-010.
5. A targeted single-cell test sweep on each lane (use any short
   TerminalBench-2.1 task — e.g. `hello-world` or similar) produces a
   report.json with reasoning_tokens populated and the value within the
   probe-verified band:
   - OR-Qwen + reasoning=low + wire=tokens: reasoning_tokens ~= 2048 ± 30%.
   - sindri-llamacpp + reasoning=low: reasoning_tokens ~= 2048 ± 30%
     (assuming the chat-template template flip happened — if not, ~= 0 and
     the template-flip is a separate operator action).
   - vidar-ds4 + reasoning=low: reasoning_tokens ~= 2048 ± 30%.
6. The full epic (fizeau-92d44ce0) closes after this bead closes.

governing.title: ADR-010: Reasoning Wire Form Belongs in the Model Catalog
